description: Has Auth0UniversalComponents package dependency (SPM, CocoaPods, or Carthage)
Uses MyAccountAuthMethodsView component for MFA management
Implements TokenProvider protocol with required methods
Uses secure credential storage via CredentialsManager
Has login/logout authentication flow for step-up auth

Does not use localhost callback URLs in native app
Does not include client secret in native mobile app

question: Does the solution correctly integrate Auth0UniversalComponents into a SwiftUI app with proper SDK initialization, TokenProvider implementation, and MyAccountAuthMethodsView embedded in a NavigationStack? This is a correctness check for the overall integration.
description: Does the solution correctly integrate Auth0UniversalComponents into a SwiftUI app
Advanced: configures My Account API audience for MFA operations
Advanced: properly handles NavigationStack requirement for MFA view
Does not use deprecated Lock library

question: Does the solution demonstrate knowledge beyond basic Auth0 Swift SDK usage — specifically: (1) Auth0UniversalComponentsSDKInitializer initialization with tokenProvider, (2) custom TokenProvider protocol implementation with fetchAPICredentials for My Account API, (3) MyAccountAuthMethodsView with NavigationStack, (4) My Account API audience configuration? Exclude basic patterns: import Auth0, Auth0.plist, webAuth, credentials, CredentialsManager. This is a skill attribution judge assessing whether the skill provided unique value.
description: Checks for skill-specific advanced pattern usage: Auth0UniversalComponentsSDKInitializer, TokenProvider, MyAccountAuthMethodsView, My Account API audience
